exercises.title: Iterate and Report Scores
exercises.description: Given a scores dictionary, iterate over .items() and print a formatted report showing each student's score and letter grade (A=90+, B=80+, C otherwise). Then print the class average.
exercises.starterCode:
scores = {"Alice": 92, "Bob": 78, "Carol": 95, "David": 81}

# TODO: Iterate over scores.items() sorted by name
# For each student, determine letter grade:
# A if score >= 90, B if score >= 80, C otherwise
# Print: "Alice       92  A"

# TODO: Calculate and print the class average
# Format: "Average: 86.5"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Alice        92  A
Bob          78  C
Carol        95  A
David        81  B
Average: 86.5
hints: for name, score in sorted(scores.items()): iterates alphabetically
grade = 'A' if score >= 90 else 'B' if score >= 80 else 'C'

exercises.title: Group Students by Department
exercises.description: Use setdefault to group a list of (name, department) pairs into a dict mapping department to a list of names. Then print each department and its sorted members.
exercises.starterCode:
students = [
    ("Alice", "CS"),
    ("Bob", "Math"),
    ("Carol", "CS"),
    ("David", "Physics"),
    ("Eve", "Math"),
    ("Frank", "CS"),
]

# TODO: Build 'by_dept' dict using setdefault
# by_dept[dept].append(name)


# TODO: Print each department and its sorted members
# Format: "CS: Alice, Carol, Frank"
exercises.expectedOutput:
CS: Alice, Carol, Frank
Math: Bob, Eve
Physics: David
hints: for dept, names in sorted(by_dept.items()): iterates alphabetically

exercises.title: Dict Comprehension: Discount Prices
exercises.description: Given a prices dictionary, use dict comprehensions to: create a discounted prices dict (20% off, rounded to 2 decimal places), and create a filtered dict with only items costing more than $1.50 after discount.
exercises.starterCode:
prices = {"apple": 1.50, "banana": 0.75, "cherry": 3.00, "date": 2.50, "elderberry": 0.50}

# TODO: Create 'discounted' dict: apply 20% discount, round to 2 decimal places

# TODO: Create 'affordable' dict: only items with discounted price > 1.50

print(discounted)
print(affordable)
hints: {k: round(v * 0.8, 2) for k, v in prices.items()} applies the discount
{k: v for k, v in discounted.items() if v > 1.50} filters the result
